docs.title: Welcome to PyPortion Docs
docs.text: PyPortion is a lightweight and developer-friendly Python CLI tool that helps you accelerate project development, keep your codebase consistent, and eliminate repetitive work.

docs.title: Key Features
docs.text:
• 🚀 Quick Start – Scaffold new projects in seconds with zero setup time
  • 🛠️ Easy Management – Maintain and organize your projects effortlessly with built-in templates
  • 📦 Clean & Consistent – Scalable architecture that stays clean, maintainable, and up to standard as you grow

docs.title: Why PyPortion?
docs.text:
• ✨ Boost your development speed
  • 🔁 Eliminate repetitive boilerplate work
  • 📏 Enforce clean and organized project structure
  • 🪶 Fast, minimal, and easy to extend

docs.title: Contributing to pyportion
docs.text: Thank you for your interest in contributing to pyportion 🎉

docs.title: Types of Contributions
docs.text:
• 🐛 Reporting bugs – Found something broken? Open an issue
  • 💡 Suggesting features – Have an idea? Start a discussion
  • 🧪 Testing new releases – Try the latest versions and share feedback
  • 🧱 Improving documentation – Help others understand pyportion
  • 🧰 Contributing code – Fix bugs or add new features

docs.title: Local Development Setup
docs.text:
In order to contribute to PyPortion code, you can follow these steps:

  1. Fork the repository Go to the GitHub repo and click "Fork" to create your own copy.

  2. Clone your fork 

    git clone https://github.com/<your-username>/pyportion

  3. Download poetry (if you don't have it) 

    curl -sSL https://install.python-poetry.org | python3 -

  4. Install project dependencies 

    poetry install

  5. Start Development

You can start the development process

  1. Install Pre-Commit One you are done from the development process, run the following: 

    poetry run pre-commit install

  2. Commit and Push your changes Use git to commit and push your changes to open a pull request

docs.title: Pre-Commit
docs.text:
Pre-Commit hooks will work locally upon comitting and do the following if needed: * Format code * Lint code * Type check * Sort imports * Run test cases

Note that it might automatically fix the files you committed to match our coding style, so you’ll need to add and commit those changes again before pushing.

docs.title: 🔄 Pull Request Review
docs.text:
All pull requests go through an automatic review via GitHub Actions, followed by a manual review.

Reviewers focus on: * Code clarity and readability * Consistency with the project structure * Test coverage * Quality and clarity of commit messages

docs.title: 💬 Need Help?
docs.text: If you’re unsure about something, feel free to open a discussion.

docs.title: Introduction
docs.text: This guide will help you to get started in PyPortion, run your first commands and create your first project in few minutes.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
1. Inspect templates
  2. Download Templates
  3. Build custom templates
  4. Use PyPortion in existing projects

docs.title: User Guide
docs.text: Welcome to the PyPortion User Guide. This section contains comprehensive documentation for anyone who wants to utilize PyPortion in their Python project development.

docs.title: What You'll Learn
docs.text:
